name: Cales Coves
description: A twin cove enclosed by cliffs honeycombed with a prehistoric necropolis of caves, a Talayotic-Roman site of considerable cultural and scenic value. There are no facilities; it pairs a quiet swim with archaeology.

highlights: swim in a twin cove ringed by cliffs honeycombed with caves
see from the water the necropolis of prehistoric caves carved into the rock
respect the site: it is a protected archaeological area
